channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Israeli military orders the evacuation of Gaza's southern city of Rafah
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israel's military on Monday issued sweeping evacuation orders covering Rafah and nearby areas, indicating it could soon launch another major ground operation in the Gaza Strip's southernmost city.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and FATMA KHALED, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Red Cross: Israel killed 8 Palestinian medics in strikes on ambulances
item.description: The Red Cross said Israel killed eight Palestinian medics when it struck ambulances in southern Gaza. United Nations officials said the Red Cross workers' bodies, along with other Palestinian rescue workers, were recovered from a grave in the sand, Reuters reported.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: US sanctions Sinaloa Cartel associates for alleged money laundering
item.description: The U.S. Treasury Department has sanctioned six people and seven companies it said were involved in money laundering for factions of the Sinaloa Cartel.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Finland's president says Trump growing 'impatient' with Putin
item.description: Finnish President Alexander Stubb said in a new interview that President Trump seemed to be growing “impatient” with Russian President Vladimir Putin, who rejected a U.S. ceasefire proposal and added various conditions to a more limited deal. Stubb, who played golf with the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: A Kremlin official says Russia sees efforts to end Ukraine war as a drawn-out process
item.description: Russia views efforts to end its three-year war with Ukraine as “a drawn-out process,” a Kremlin spokesman said Monday, after U.S. President Donald Trump expressed frustration with the two countries’ leaders as he tries to bring about a truce.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Putin open to contacts after Trump criticism: Kremlin
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin says he's open to staying in touch with the White House after critical comments from the U.S. president toward Russia's leader, which have been rare amid his efforts to get peace talks rolling.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: 60 percent disapprove of Trump tariffs: Survey
item.description: A new poll found that nearly 60% of Americans disagree with President Trump's handling of trade negotiations, with only 38% approving of his trade policies and 40% approving of his overall economic approach.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, The Hill

item.title: What to know about the ruling barring Marine Le Pen from seeking public office for five years
item.description: PARIS (AP) — French far-right leader Marine Le Pen has been sentenced to a five-year ban on running for public office for embezzling EU funds, a major political earthquake. The ruling, which takes effect immediately, is likely to bar her from running in France’s next presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Iran's Khamenei warns of 'strong blow' as Trump threatens to drop bombs, Putin silent on US ire
item.description: Iran and Russia took decisively different approaches in response to President Donald Trump's threats as Tehran vowed action against any "mischief" while the Kremlin remains tight-lipped.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bangkok reviewing construction safety rules after high-rise building's collapse during earthquake
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Thai authorities said Monday they are investigating why a single office tower block under construction in Bangkok collapsed during Friday's earthquake that otherwise caused limited damage in the capital. Bangkok Gov.
item.dc:creator: JERRY HARMER, JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI and ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Klobuchar: ‘Israel was incredibly angry’ about Signal leak
item.description: Sen. Amy Klobuchar (D-Minn.) said Israel was “incredibly angry” about the plans to attack Houthi rebels in Yemen leaked in a Signal group chat with Trump administration officials and a journalist. Klobuchar joined MSNBC’s Jen Psaki on Sunday, where she weighed in on the ongoing scandal.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Trump says ‘terrorists who attacked the beautiful Turnberry’ have been captured
item.description: President Trump said he was informed that the “terrorists who attacked" his Turnberry golf resort in Scotland had been captured.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Declining Eid travel and spending in Indonesia and discrimination in India dampen holiday spirit
item.description: JAKARTA, Indonesia (AP) — The usual festive mood of Eid al-Fitr holiday to mark the end of the Islamic holy month of Ramadan has been subdued in Indonesia this year as people grapple with soaring prices for food, clothing and essential goods.
item.dc:creator: NINIEK KARMINI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Greenland prime minister says US will not get Greenland
item.description: The prime minister of Greenland pushed back Sunday against assertions by U.S. President Donald Trump that America will take control of the island territory. Greenland, a huge, resource-rich island in the Atlantic, is a self-governing territory of Denmark, a NATO ally of the United States.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Smell of death permeates Myanmar cities after quake kills over 1,600 and leaves countless buried
item.description: MANDALAY, Myanmar (AP) — The smell of decaying bodies permeated the streets of Myanmar's second-largest city on Sunday as people worked frantically by hand to clear rubble in the hope of finding someone still alive, two days after a massive earthquake struck that killed more than 1,600 people and le
item.dc:creator: THEIN ZAW, DAVID RISING and GRANT PECK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: What to know about Myanmar's civil war, which is complicating earthquake relief efforts
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — The Southeast Asian nation of Myanmar has been plagued by wars and natural disasters since it became independent from Britain in 1948, when it was still called Burma.
item.dc:creator: GRANT PECK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Myanmar quake death toll rises to 1,644 as resistance movement announces partial ceasefire
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A unilateral partial ceasefire to facilitate earthquake relief efforts was announced on Saturday by Myanmar’s shadow National Unity Government, which coordinates the popular struggle against the ruling military. The country's death toll from the disaster soared to 1,644.
item.dc:creator: GRANT PECK and JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hamas says it accepts a new Gaza ceasefire proposal but Israel makes a counter-offer
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — The Hamas militant group said Saturday it has accepted a new Gaza ceasefire proposal from mediators Egypt and Qatar, but Israel said it has made a counter-proposal in “full coordination” with the third mediator, the United States.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Faye Hall, American detained by Taliban, has been released
item.description: Faye Hall, an American woman who had been detained by the Taliban since February, has been freed, a source with knowledge of the release told Fox News Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Latest: Countries sending humanitarian aid after Myanmar earthquake
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — The death toll from Myanmar's powerful 7.7 magnitude earthquake keeps climbing amid rescue efforts. The military government said Saturday that 1,644 people have been killed, with thousands of others injured and dozens missing.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Doctor cites the pope's 'surprising improvement' after surviving life-threatening crises
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis has shown ‘’a truly surprising improvement’’ since returning to the Vatican to convalesce after surviving a life-threatening bout with double-pneumonia, the doctor who coordinated the pontiff’s five-week hospitalization said Saturday. “I find him very lively,’’ Dr.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN BARRY and TRISHA THOMAS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: UK bans ninja swords in move to crack down on violent knife crime
item.description: As knife crime continues to plague the U.K., the Labour government is attempting to crack down by adding ninja swords to the list of banned knives.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel strikes Beirut for the first time since a ceasefire ended the latest Israel-Hezbollah war
item.description: Israel has launched an attack on Beirut, marking its first strike on Lebanon's capital since a ceasefire ended the latest Israel-Hezbollah war last November.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Powerful earthquake rocks Thailand and Myanmar, kills more than 150
item.description: A 7.7 magnitude earthquake rocked Thailand and neighboring Myanmar midday on Friday, burying an unknown number when a high-rise building under construction collapsed.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Missing US soldiers in Lithuania killed: NATO chief
item.description: Four U.S. soldiers who went missing while training in Lithuania have died, NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte said Wednesday. Speaking to reporters during a trip to Warsaw, Rutte said he had received word of the deaths of the four American troops.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, NewsNation

item.title: The Atlantic releases full Signal text chain
item.description: The Atlantic editor-in-chief on Wednesday released the full text message chain between Trump administration officials.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation
